folio.label: 74v
folio.l: Lokyng out at his dore , vp on a day
A nother Romayne , told he me by name
That for his wyf , was at a someres game
Witú outen his wetyngö , he forsoke hire eke
And than wold he , vp on his bible seke
That ilke ¸uerbe , of ecclesiaste
Where he comaundetú , & forbedetú faste
Man shal nat suffre his wyf , go royle a boute
Than wold he sey rigút thus , w oute doute
¢ Who so that bildetú his hous , al of salwes
And priketú his blynd hors , ouer the falwes
And suffretú his wyf , to seken halwes
Is worthy to ben hanged , on the galwes
But al for nougút , I sette nougút an hawe
Of hise ¸uerbes , ne of his olde lawe
Ne I wold nat of hi~ , corrected be
I hate hi~ , that my vices telletú me
And so don moo of vs , god wot , than I
This mad hi~ witú me wood , al vtterly
I wold nat forbere hi~ , in no cas
Now wyl I sey ©ow sotú , by Seynt Thomas
Why that I rent out , of his book a leef
For whiche he smot me so , that I was deef
He had a bok , that gladly nygút and day
For his desport , he wold it rede alway
He cleped it valerie , and Theofaste
At whicú book , he lougú alwey ful fast´
And eke there was somtyme , a Clerk at Rome
A Cardynale that higút , Seynt Ierome
That mad a book , a geyn Iovynyan
In whiche book eke , there was Tertulan
Crisippus , Tortula , and helowys
That was Abbesse , nat fer fro Parys
And eke the parobles , of Salamou~
Ouydes art , and bookes many oon
And alle theise were bounden , in o volume
And euäy nygút & day , was his custume
Whan he had leiser , and vacacione
From other worldly , occupacione
To redyn in this book , of wykked wyues
He knew of hem , moo legendes & lyues
Than ben of goode wyues , in the bible
For trostetú wel , it is an inpossible
That any Clerk , wyl speke good of wyues
But if it be , of holy Seyntes lyues
Ne of noone othere wo~men , neuäe the moo
